highlight.title: Cloud Run Gets Multi-Container Support and GPU Preview
highlight.summary: Google Cloud Run now supports multiple containers (sidecars) for jobs in preview, enabling more complex workload patterns. Additionally, GPU configuration is available in preview, opening up ML/AI workload possibilities on the serverless platform.

resources.title: Cloud Run Introduces Flexible CUD Pricing and Manual Scaling
resources.why_now: Cloud Run now supports flexible committed use discounts for CPU-allocated services and jobs, plus manual scaling options. This directly impacts cost optimization strategies for production workloads.

resources.title: Secret Manager Cost Optimization with Cache-First Patterns
resources.why_now: With Secret Manager charging per access operation beyond 10K monthly, implementing local caching patterns becomes critical for high-traffic applications to avoid unexpected bills.

resources.title: Event-Driven Architecture Patterns with Pub/Sub Dead Letter Queues
resources.why_now: Recent guide demonstrates practical dead letter queue implementation for Pub/Sub, essential for reliable event-driven systems that handle failures gracefully in production.

resources.title: App Engine Standard Migration to Cloud Run in Preview
resources.why_now: The `gcloud beta app migrate-to-run` command is now available in preview, providing a migration path for teams looking to modernize from App Engine to Cloud Run's more flexible platform.

claude_take: The serverless ecosystem is maturing with Cloud Run gaining enterprise features like multi-container support and flexible pricing, while observability tools like Secret Manager require careful cost management as applications scale. The migration tooling from App Engine to Cloud Run signals Google's strategic direction toward containerized serverless.
